status: PARTIAL — only Router deployed; SY adapters + Factory blocked on Hedera msg.value-to-precompile-from-constructor issue (see notes)

blocker.issue: SYBase.constructor calls HtsHelpers.createFungible(spec, decimals) which forwards msg.value to the 0x167 precompile. On Hedera mainnet (both via Hashio Ethereum-tx adapter AND via @hashgraph/sdk ContractCreate+setInitialBalance), the child TOKENCREATION transaction spawned by the precompile call has max_fee=0 → INSUFFICIENT_TX_FEE → CONTRACT_REVERT_EXECUTED.
evidence: tx 0.0.995584-1777917303-019174269 (Hashio ETHEREUMTRANSACTION, tx.value=2 HBAR): TOKENCREATION max_fee=0
blocker.rootCause: msg.value forwarded to a precompile call doesn't fund the spawned child HAPI tx. This pattern works in the local revm tests (mock 0x167 doesn't spawn HAPI children) but breaks on real Hedera consensus.
blocker.fix: Refactor SYBase to defer HTS token creation out of the constructor. Two paths: (a) two-step init — constructor stores name/symbol, separate `initShareToken()` payable function creates HTS post-deploy; (b) `SYDeployer` contract that holds HBAR balance and orchestrates SY + HTS token in one external call. Either requires test updates.

spentSoFar: ~114 HBAR on failed deploys (Factory 2× attempts, SY 3× attempts)
remaining: 353 HBAR — sufficient once the SYBase refactor lands
